volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Casket Comedy (Section 4.2.749 - Section 5.1.785)

sections.label: Section 4.2.749
sections.translation: This daughter of my mistress had them .

sections.label: Section 4.2.750
sections.original: Mentiris , nam mea gestitavit , non tua .
sections.translation: You tell a falsehood ; for my own mistress ’ s daughter had them , not yours .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751
sections.translation: Don ’ t interrupt .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751b
sections.translation: I ’ ll be mum .

sections.label: Section 4.2.751c
sections.translation: Good woman , go on speaking . Where is she who had them ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.752b
sections.translation: Here , next door .

sections.label: Section 4.2.753
sections.original: Istic quidem edepol mei virí habitat gener .
sections.translation: By the powers , surely the son-in-law of my husband is living there .

sections.label: Section 4.2.753b
sections.original: Ne obloquere rusus . perge porro dicere .
sections.translation: Interrupting again ? ( To HALISCA .) Go on relating it . How many years old is she said to be ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.755b
sections.translation: Seventeen .

sections.label: Section 4.2.756
sections.translation: She is my own daughter then !

sections.label: Section 4.2.756b
sections.original: East : u ⋯ numer us annorum attulit .
sections.translation: ’ Tis she , as the number of her years has proved .

sections.label: Section 4.2.757
sections.original: Quid qua tibi causa haec dixi ? iam quaero meam .
sections.translation: What you are seeking , you have found ; I now seek what ’ s mine .

sections.label: Section 4.2.758
sections.original: At po l ego ⋯ sunt , quaero tertiam .
sections.translation: Why , faith , they ’ ve found what ’ s their own , I ’ ll seek for number three [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.759
sections.translation: My daughter , the object which I was seeking , I have discovered .

sections.label: Section 4.2.760
sections.original: Aequom est reponi per fidem quod creditum est ,
sections.translation: It ’ s proper to keep in safety what has been entrusted in confidence , lest a kindness should turn out a detriment to the well-deserving . This fosterling of ours is assuredly your daughter ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.762b
sections.original: et redditura est tuam tibi , et ea gratia
sections.translation: and my mistress is about to restore you your own , and for that purpose has she come from her house . But , prithee ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.765
sections.translation: enquire of her own self ; I am but a servant .

sections.label: Section 4.2.765b
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s just .

sections.label: Section 4.2.766
sections.original: Illius égo istanc esse malo gratiam .
sections.translation: To her rather do I choose this obligation to belong . But I beg that you ’ ll restore me that casket .

sections.label: Section 4.2.768
sections.translation: What ’ s to be done , Lampadio ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.768b
sections.translation: What ’ s your own , keep as your own .

sections.label: Section 4.2.769
sections.translation: But I feel compassion for her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.769b
sections.translation: This I think ought to be done ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.770
sections.original: da isti cistellam et intro abi cum istac semul .
sections.translation: give her the casket , and go in-doors together with her .

sections.label: Section 4.2.771
sections.original: Tibi auscultabo . tene tu cistellam tibi ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow your advice . ( Giving it to HALISCA .) Take you the casket . Let ’ s go in-doors . But what ’ s the name of your mistress ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.773b
sections.translation: Melaenis .

sections.label: Section 4.2.773c
sections.original: I prae , iám ego te sequar .—
sections.translation: Go first ; I ’ ll follow you at once .

sections.label: Section 5.1.774
sections.original: Quíd hoc negoti est , quód omnes homines fabulantur per vias
sections.translation: What affair is this , that all persons are talking about in the street — that my daughter has been found ? They say , too , that Lampadio [*] has been seeking me in the Forum .

sections.label: Section 5.1.776b
sections.translation: Master , whence come you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.776c
sections.translation: From the Senate .

sections.label: Section 5.1.776d
sections.translation: I rejoice that through my means there is an addition to your children .

sections.label: Section 5.1.777b
sections.translation: But it don ’ t please me ; I don ’ t want that I should be having more children by means of another person . But what is the meaning of this ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.779b
sections.original: Propera ire intro húc ad adfinem tuom ,
sections.translation: Make haste , and go in-doors here to the house of your neighbour ; you ’ ll at once recognize your daughter . Your wife ’ s in-doors there as well . Go quickly .

sections.label: Section 5.1.781b
sections.original: Praevorti hoc certumst rebus aliis omnibus .—
sections.translation: I ’ m resolved that this shall , before all other matters , be attended to .

sections.label: Section 5.1.782
sections.original: Ne exspectetis , spectatores , dum illi huc ad vos exeant :
sections.translation: Don ’ t you wait , Spectators , till they come out to you ; no one will come out ; they ’ ll all finish the business indoors ; when that shall be done , they ’ ll lay aside their dress ; then , after that ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.785
sections.original: qui deliquit vapulabit , qui non deliquit bibet .
sections.translation: he that has done amiss will get a beating [*] ; he that has not done amiss will get some drink . Now as to what ’ s left , Spectators , for you to do , after the manner of your ancestors , give your applause at the conclusion of the Play .
